titlestmt.titleproper: Xth International Congress, History of Science, Ithaca-Philadelphia records14-17-645
titlestmt.author: Compiled by

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-01-12 23:02:06 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Xth International Congress, History of Science, Ithaca-Philadelphia records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: International Congress on the History of Sciences (10th ) (Location of meeting: Ithaca, N.Y. and Philadelphia, Penn.). Date of meeting or treaty signing: 1962 :.)

did.abstract: Correspondence and files relating to the Congress held at Ithaca.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: International Congress of the History of Science records, #14-17-645. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and files relating to the Congress held at Ithaca.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also photographs (with identifications) taken in the History of Science Collections and the Rare Book Department in Olin Library at Cornell University by C. Hadley Smith.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of the office of origin.

did.unittitle: Journals that published announcements and did not
Letters informing pulled from program, answering requests for general info.
I - General problems in the history of science; methods, philosophy and historiography of science
II - History of Technology and Applied Science
IV - Science in the Middle Ages and the Renaissance
V. 2. Histroy of Physics and Astronomy
Section V. 3. History of Chemistry (including pharmacy)
VI. 1. General Problems in the History of Science; Methods, Philosophy and Histriography of Science
VII. Sciences of Man (Psycology, Anthropology, Sociology, Linguistics)
C.D.H. Correspondence to H.G. and H.G. to C.D.H.
Housing and Meals - Whiting and Shaw Ryon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also, 2 3x5" card file drawers, one containing a list of libraries, museums, colleges, etc. in the U.S. and Canada and the other, a list of domestic and foreign periodicals

did.unittitle: Sources for Funds - Corning Glass Foundation
